name: Lat Pulldown
steps: Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width, palms facing away
Pull the bar down toward your upper chest
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom
Slowly let the bar rise back up with control
common_mistake: Pulling with your arms instead of your back. Think about driving your elbows down toward your hips rather than pulling with your hands.

name: Face Pulls
steps: Set a cable machine to upper chest height with a rope attachment
Pull the rope toward your face, separating your hands as you pull
Keep your elbows high and parallel to the ground
Slowly return to the starting position with control
common_mistake: Letting your elbows drop down. Keep them up the whole time or you end up working your biceps instead of your rear delts.

name: Overhead Press
steps: Hold the bar or dumbbells at shoulder height, elbows slightly in front
Press straight up until your arms are fully extended
Keep your core tight and avoid arching your lower back
Lower the weight back to shoulder height with control
common_mistake: Flaring your elbows out too wide. Keep them at about a 45 degree angle to protect your shoulders and get a stronger press.

name: Preacher Curl
steps: Sit at the preacher bench and grip the EZ bar at the angled part
Start with arms almost fully extended over the pad
Curl the bar up toward your shoulders
Slowly lower it back down without letting your arms drop completely
common_mistake: Using momentum to swing the bar up. The pad is there to isolate your biceps so keep your arms pressed against it the whole time.

name: Tricep Pushdown
steps: Set a cable machine to the highest position with a straight bar or rope attachment
Grip the bar with palms facing down and tuck your elbows at your sides
Push the bar straight down until your arms are fully extended
Slowly let the bar rise back up, keeping your elbows locked in place
common_mistake: Letting your elbows flare out or move forward as you push down. Keep them pinned to your sides the whole time or you take the tension off your triceps.

name: Close Grip Cable Row
steps: Sit at the cable row machine and grip the close grip handle with both hands
Keep your back straight and your chest up as you pull the handle toward your stomach
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement
Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position with control
common_mistake: Rounding your lower back as you pull. Keep your torso upright and avoid leaning too far forward or backward during the movement.

name: Barbell Bent Over Row
steps: Stand with feet shoulder width apart and hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder width with palms facing down
Pull the bar toward your lower chest, driving your elbows back and up
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with control
common_mistake: Using your lower back to yank the bar up instead of pulling with your back muscles. Keep your core braced and your torso still throughout the movement.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
steps: Stand with a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, palms facing inward
With a slight bend in your elbows, raise both arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor
Pause briefly at the top of the movement
Slowly lower the dumbbells back down to your sides with control
common_mistake: Using momentum to swing the weights up instead of lifting with your side delts. Use a lighter weight and focus on a slow controlled raise rather than swinging.

name: Hammer Curl
steps: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing inward toward your body
Keep your elbows tucked at your sides and curl both dumbbells up toward your shoulders
Pause at the top and squeeze your biceps
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control
common_mistake: Swinging your elbows forward or using your back to help lift the weight. Keep your elbows pinned to your sides throughout the entire movement.
